mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reflects tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in declining unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the federal funds rate above a 0.25 percentage point monthly threshold signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized expansion in residential investment and consumer spending, which lifts aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index below the 100 level during a late-cycle expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which erodes corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions do not predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p-value greater than 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue streams and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which triggers risk-off positioning and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rapid unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases across historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and elevated federal funds rates indicate a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation combined with high rates fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales forecasts immediate consumer spending weakness that reduces near-term corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during periods of elevated consumer debt.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not predict negative 20-day returns when consumer debt-to-income ratios are below historical medians.
